isolation.notes: Public smoke checks only - no authentication required

assertions.issues: Partial verification: Public port tests confirm internal services are not exposed (connection refused on all 808x ports). Full verification requires SSH access to Node B to run 'ss -tlnp | grep -E 808[1-5]' to confirm services bind to 127.0.0.1 only.

assertions.title: Proxy health endpoint reports upstream sandbox status

steps.expected: Health endpoint exposed through Caddy with status and upstream fields
steps.observed: HTTP 404 - {"error":"not found"} - endpoint not exposed publicly

steps.expected: Direct access to proxy health endpoint showing upstream status
steps.observed: Cannot test from public origin - service bound to localhost only

publicEndpointTest.conclusion: Health endpoint not exposed on public origin

internalServiceBehavior.upstreamField: Reports 'ok' when sandbox reachable, 'unreachable' when down
internalServiceBehavior.note: As documented: proxy health reports degraded/upstream status but is only accessible on localhost

assertions.issues: Partial verification: The proxy health endpoint is not exposed publicly (returns 404), which is the expected security configuration. According to restart-readiness.md, the proxy health endpoint at 127.0.0.1:8082/health is designed to be accessed internally only. Full verification requires SSH to Node B to test 'curl http://127.0.0.1:8082/health' and verify upstream field shows sandbox status.

frictions.description: VAL-DEPLOY-008: /api/health endpoint is not exposed through Caddy on the public origin - it returns 404. This is consistent with the defense-in-depth design where health endpoints are localhost-only.
frictions.resolution: Verified this is expected behavior per restart-readiness.md - proxy health at 127.0.0.1:8082/health is not meant to be public. Caddy only routes /api/* through the proxy, but the proxy itself only exposes health on its local bind address.

blockers.description: SSH access to Node B not available for this validation session
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Attempted only public-origin checks as specified in task. Full verification requires 'ssh node-b' access to run 'ss -tlnp' and test localhost health endpoints directly.

summary: Tested 2 assertions via public-origin curl checks: both marked partial. VAL-DEPLOY-007: All internal service ports (8081-8085) correctly blocked from public origin - connection refused/timeout confirms defense-in-depth. VAL-DEPLOY-008: Proxy health endpoint not exposed publicly (404) - this is expected as it binds to localhost per security design. Full verification of both assertions requires SSH access to Node B to verify localhost binding via 'ss -tlnp' and test proxy health response showing upstream sandbox status.
